```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD)
}

lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); 
    c.kind == x.kind &&
    c.value == x.value &&
    c.width >= PAD &&
    (x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width) &&
    Wellformed(c) &&
    (Wellformed(x) && x.width >= PAD ==> Canonicalize(x) == x)
{ }
```

The implementation is straightforward:
- `Canonicalize` preserves the `kind` and `value` fields unchanged
- It sets the `width` to be the maximum of the current width and `PAD` (4), ensuring minimum 4-digit padding
- If already at 4+ digits, the width stays the same (no-op)

The specification ensures:
1. Kind and value are preserved
2. Result has width >= PAD (minimum 4 digits)
3. If input width >= PAD, output width equals input width (unchanged)
4. Result is wellformed
5. Canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op (idempotent)
